title: Fix problem with `podman machine list` returning wrong units for Memory and Disk size
body:
units.BytesSize expects byte size as argument.
Therefore, added code to convert vm.Memory and vm.DiskSize to byte size using strongunits.

Fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21917

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
Fix problem with `podman machine list` returning wrong units for Memory and Disk size
```

comments.body: There should probably be a regression test added for this somewhere, so that it doesn't happen again?
No I am saying the point of strongunit is that we know what the number represents. So the logic thing is to carry the type change up the stack, i.e. `vm.Memory` already should be a strongunit type.
Code LGTM. Lint is angry about a lack of tests.
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] was dropped recently (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21891) so it can no longer be used we now have to add the label which can only be done by repo maintainers.
Could you squash your commits down to one, changes itself LGTM
